name: LIQUID GLASS - frosted depth-layered interface
ref: Apple Liquid Glass / WWDC 2026 (Art Directors Club Gold Cube winner); Exo Ape 'Fluid Glass' site (CSS Design Awards 2026)
palette: #0d0d0f (near-black base) + #e8f4ff (ice-white glass accent); blur and tint are the color system, not fills
brief: Multiple translucent glass panes float at distinct z-depths over a slowly drifting background gradient; each pane hosts one work card with frosted backdrop-filter blur creating genuine depth hierarchy. Scrolling pushes foreground panes at a faster rate than background ones (true parallax without fake perspective tricks). Hover on a pane sharpens its blur to zero and brightens the tint - the pane slides toward you. The design IS the depth system; no solid fills, no borders, just blur and layered shadow. Mobile: single-pane stack with swipe-to-advance; blur radius reduced for GPU budget. Guardrail: backdrop-filter is GPU-bounded; cap at 3 concurrent blurred panes and skip the effect behind reduced-motion.

name: CCTV - grainy surveillance monitor grid
ref: NGR.EV surveillance aesthetic (aigoodies 2026 trend report); Jean Pierre Consuegra security-UI event posters (2026)
palette: #0c0c0c (monitor black) + #39ff14 (phosphor green); grain noise overlay at 20% opacity
brief: Works are presented as if on a bank of analogue security monitors: each frame has a VHS timestamp overlay, faint scanlines, and a continuous subtle noise grain shader running on a canvas element. The grid layout is intentionally uneven (2+3+2 rows) suggesting a real security room. Clicking a monitor maximises it with a satisfying CRT-zoom flicker, while the others shrink to thumbnails with increased grain. A blinking REC dot and rolling timecode are the only permanent UI chrome. Mobile: single-monitor view with a strip of mini-monitors below; swipe to cycle. Performance: grain is a single canvas overlay, not per-element - one rAF loop for the whole page.

name: AXIS SHIFT - vertical scroll triggers horizontal slide
ref: Nikola Radeski portfolio (scrollytelling.ai top examples 2026); Igloo.inc fully immersive navigation redesign (metabole.studio 2026)
palette: #f5f0e8 (warm paper) + #c0392b (vermilion); axis transition is a one-color wipe
brief: The page begins as a standard vertical scroll; at a threshold point the scroll axis rotates 90 degrees - a perspective CSS transform signals the pivot and the content lane switches to horizontal locomotion without any JS scroll hijacking (uses native scroll-driven animations and CSS scroll-snap). Works are arranged in a long horizontal row accessed through this rotated lane, then vertical scroll re-engages at the end to exit. The axis moment itself is the signature: a brief 3D tilt that literally 'turns the corner'. Mobile: the horizontal lane collapses to full-width swipeable snap points. Guardrail: the pivot animation is CSS-only, no GSAP, so no render-blocking JS on the critical path.

name: STAMP DECK - interactive postcard/stamp inspection gallery
ref: Codrops 'Interactive Digital Stamp Collection with Shaders' (June 2026 featured); Terminal Industries wireframe-reveal technique (metabole.studio 2026)
palette: #faf6ee (aged paper) + #2c1654 (postal ink); perforated stamp border = accent
brief: Works are presented as oversized postage stamps arranged in a collector's grid; each stamp has a perforated edge (CSS mask with radial cutouts), a denomination glyph, and a cancellation mark overlay. Hovering uses a WebGL magnifying-loupe shader (barrel distortion + chromatic aberration at the lens edge) to inspect fine detail - the loupe follows the cursor. Clicking 'pins' the stamp and peels back to reveal the full-res work underneath with a satisfying curl animation. On mobile the loupe becomes a pinch-zoom gesture; stamps re-arrange from collector grid to horizontal scroll strip. Performance: the loupe shader is a single small postprocessing pass on a canvas overlay; inactive stamps are plain IMG elements.

name: DESKTOP CORE - drag-able OS windows on a faux desktop
ref: Milosz Chudy portfolio (aigoodies 2026 Interface Nostalgia trend); Player Zero AI 'desktop UI' aesthetic (aigoodies 2026)
brief: The entire page is a faux operating system desktop circa 1995-2001: draggable window panels (CSS + pointer events, no heavy lib), a taskbar with a blinking clock, right-click context menus, and works displayed inside maximisable app windows that overlap naturally. Double-click an icon to open a work; minimise sends it to the taskbar. The nostalgia is precise and playful, not glitchy or ironic - clean skeuomorphic UI with real pixel fonts (Press Start 2P or Perfect DOS VGA). Mobile: the desktop collapses to a phone-home-screen grid of app icons that open full-screen work modals. Guardrail: all drag logic is native pointer-events + transform; no heavy framework, 0 extra deps.

name: PRESSURE - variable-font weight driven by cursor pressure / scroll velocity
ref: Obys Agency kinetic typography (metabole.studio 2026); AVA SRG and Blux Studio variable type animation (envato 2026 trend report)
palette: #f8f8f5 (off-white) + #111111 (near-black); typography alone carries the design; zero decorative colour
brief: A large-type layout where all headings and labels are set in a variable font; the font-weight axis is driven by cursor speed (fast move = ultra-heavy 900wt, still = hairline 100wt) and the width axis responds to scroll velocity (fast scroll = compressed, slow = extended). Works appear as captions beneath expanding type - the text IS the navigation. Clicking the active heading expands the work full screen with a weight-to-normal transition. The entire visual language emerges from one font's axes; no imagery is required in the frame itself - works are revealed only on demand. Mobile: weight responds to scroll speed; swipe-fast for bold, slow for thin. Performance: variable font renders entirely in CSS; no canvas, no WebGL.

name: CHAPTER BOOK - persistent sidebar chapter-jump with scroll-sync prose
ref: Adidas Annual Report 2024 persistent sidebar navigation (scrollytelling.ai 2026); SBS 'The Boat' audio-visual intensity sync (scrollytelling.ai 2026)
palette: #1a1a2e (deep editorial navy) + #e8c547 (chapter gold); serif body + monospace chapter labels
brief: A long-form editorial layout: a fixed left sidebar shows chapter numbers and short labels; clicking jumps the main content lane to that chapter with a smooth scroll-hijack-free anchor. Each chapter has its own scroll-triggered reveal mechanic (fade-in text, image wipe, a counter ticking up) that is unique to that chapter's content type. A reading-progress arc around the chapter dot shows how far through that chapter the reader is. The works themselves appear inline within the prose as evidence, not as gallery items. On mobile the sidebar collapses to a floating dot-nav row at the bottom. Guardrail: all chapter animations are CSS scroll-driven (no ScrollTrigger); the sidebar is position:sticky, zero layout shift.

name: BIOLUMINESCENT - organic mesh gradient that breathes
ref: Microsoft.ai 'soft palette vegetal shaders' (metabole.studio 2026); Ylem.art tech-organic fusion (aigoodies 2026 trend report)
palette: #030a12 (abyss black) + #00f5a0 (bioluminescent teal); secondary glow #8a2be2 (deep violet) only inside shader
brief: The background is a full-viewport GLSL mesh gradient that slowly morphs like a deep-sea creature breathing: sine-wave vertex displacement + perlin noise in the fragment shader produces an aurora-borealis-meets-bioluminescence effect. Works are mounted on dark frosted cards that sit in the glow. The mesh responds to cursor position - moving the mouse creates a ripple in the nearest gradient node like disturbing water. No per-frame DOM writes; all motion is in the shader uniform. Mobile: a static CSS radial gradient approximation is used instead of WebGL when GPU is constrained (feature-detect via canvas.getContext). Guardrail: shader runs at device pixel ratio 1 regardless of screen DPR; upscaled by the browser.

name: SCRAPBOOK - mixed-media collage with tape, stamps, and handwriting
ref: Jackie Hu portfolio / KidCore collage trend (aigoodies 2026); Toilet Paper Barcelona magazine mixed-media maximalism (aigoodies 2026)
palette: #f4f0e0 (scrapbook cream) + #d62828 (washi tape red); torn-edge masks + coffee-ring noise are the texture system
brief: Works appear as if physically pasted onto a scrapbook page: torn-paper CSS clip-path edges, washi-tape strips holding cards at slight angles, handwritten SVG annotation labels, and a subtle paper-texture noise overlay. The composition is intentionally non-grid with overlapping elements and deliberate white gaps. Scrolling reveals new scrapbook spreads; each spread has its own collage arrangement. Hover lifts a card with a subtle drop-shadow increase and slight rotation correction (as if unsticking tape). On mobile the page becomes a single-column vertical scroll with each card having a tape strip at the top. Performance: all textures are inline SVG or CSS; no raster texture fetches; clip-paths are pure CSS.

name: HOLODECK - Y3K holographic foil surfaces and alien typography
ref: FVCKRENDER 3D holographic landscapes (aigoodies 2026 Y3K trend); Mugler tech couture digital presentation (aigoodies 2026); Gufram WebGL retro-typography (wavespace 2026)
palette: #06000a (void black) + holographic rainbow via CSS conic-gradient at 60% saturation; accent solid #ff2d78 (hot magenta)
brief: Works are mounted on cards with a CSS conic-gradient holographic-foil finish (rotating hue via @property animation) that shifts with cursor tilt (using deviceOrientation on mobile, mouse delta on desktop). The typography is an alien/Y3K display font (Bebas Neue or a custom variable face with exaggerated contrast) at oversized scale. Transition between works uses a holographic lens-flare wipe: a hard-light gradient sweeps across the card before the next one reveals. The entire page feels like a physical trading card or an NFT display case come to life. Mobile: DeviceOrientation drives the foil tilt automatically as the phone is tilted. Guardrail: conic-gradient + @property is pure CSS; the tilt effect degrades gracefully to a static gradient when Orientation API is unavailable.
